title: Find base and height on a triangle
description: Identify the height of a triangle as the segment perpendicular to the base and reaching the other vertex.
topic_path: Module 5: Area, surface area, and volume problems
Topic A: Area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons
Find base and height on a triangle

assessment_items.question_content:
A base is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which line segment shows the height that corresponds to the given base of the triangle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The height of a triangle extends from the base to the vertex opposite the base at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the base?

Line segment $\purpleD{\text{C}}$ is perpendicular to the base.  

[[☃ image 1]]
So, $\purpleD{\text{C}}$ is the height.

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. An arrow shows the length of one side of the triangle is labeled base. The other two sides are labeled, in clockwise order, A and B. The dashed line is labeled C.

assessment_items.question_content:
A height is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which line segment shows the base that corresponds to the given height of the triangle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The base of a triangle is the side that meets the height at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the height?

Line segment $\blueE{\text{A}}$ is perpendicular to the height.  

[[☃ image 1]]
So, $\blueE{\text{A}}$ is the base.

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. The base is labeled A. The other two sides are labeled, in clockwise order, B and C. The dashed line is labeled height.

assessment_items.question_content:
A height is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which line segment shows the base that corresponds to the given height of the triangle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The base of a triangle is the side that meets the height at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the height?

Line segment $\goldE{\text{A}}$ is perpendicular to the height.  

[[☃ image 1]]
So, $\goldE{\text{A}}$ is the base.

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. The base is labeled A. The other two sides are labeled, in clockwise order, B and C. The dashed line is labeled height.

assessment_items.question_content:
A base is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which line segment shows the height that corresponds to the given base of the triangle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The height of a triangle extends from the base to the vertex opposite the base at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the base?

Line segment $\maroonD{\text{B}}$ is perpendicular to the base.  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
So, $\maroonD{\text{B}}$ is the height.

options.alt: A right triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to a side from the side to the opposite vertex. An arrow shows the length of one side of the triangle is labeled base. The other two sides are labeled, in clockwise order, A and B. The dashed line is labeled C.

assessment_items.question_content:
A height is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The base of a triangle is the side that meets the height at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the height?

Line segment $\goldE{\text{C}}$ is perpendicular to the height.  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
So, $\goldE{\text{C}}$ is the base.

options.alt: A right triangle that is tilted.  The legs are labeled, in clockwise order, A and B.  The longest side is labeled C. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to side C from side C to the opposite vertex. The dashed line is labeled height.

assessment_items.question_content:
A height is labeled on the triangle below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which line segment shows the base that corresponds to the given height of the triangle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The height of a triangle meets the base (or an extension of the base) at a $90^\circ$ angle.

The height and the base are perpendicular.    

Which line segment is perpendicular to the height?

Line segment $\blueE{\text{C}}$ is perpendicular to the height.  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
So, $\blueE{\text{C}}$ is the base.

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the top vertex.  The bottom side length is labeled C.  The other two sides are labeled, in clockwise order, A and B. The dashed line is labeled height.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the base to the corresponding height.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The height of a triangle extends from the base to the vertex opposite the base at a $90^\circ$ angle.

So, we need to select a height that is perpendicular to each base.

The following images show the height that corresponds with each base:

Base 2:

Base 3:
